raw_response: {"rating": 7.5, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report written with strong and consistent right-leaning framing throughout the author's own prose. The piece uncritically advances a pro-deportation, anti-immigration narrative using loaded language, one-sided sourcing, and dismissive characterizations of Democrats and their policies. The density and consistency of directional bias warrants a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own framing treats mass deportations as unambiguously beneficial, with the headline-style assertion 'Mass deportations = lower housing costs for Americans' presented as factual rather than contested; (2) The author describes Biden's immigration policy as a 'flood' of migrants and attributes rising costs directly and causally to those migrants without engaging any counterarguments or alternative economic explanations in their own prose; (3) Democrats are described as fueling 'chaos' in 'migration-swelled, crime-ridden' cities — these are the author's own characterizations, not attributed to sources, and they frame Democratic governance as destructive; (4) Seattle's mayor is characterized by the author as representing 'far-left Democrats,' a politically loaded descriptor that goes beyond neutral identification; (5) The author states that 'crime and drug deaths are also declining' and 'Americans' wages and wealth-generating productivity are rising' as direct results of Trump's deportation policies, asserting causal relationships without evidence or qualification in the author's own voice; (6) The author frames Trump's deportation policies as helping Trump 'rebut the Democrats' 2026 media-amplified affordability talking point,' framing Democratic concerns as mere talking points amplified by a biased media; (7) Source selection is entirely one-sided: all named sources (Kevin Lynn of U.S. Tech Workers, Scott Bessent, JD Vance, Scott Turner) are either Trump administration officials or immigration restriction advocates, with no contrary economic voices included; (8) The author references 'Breitbart News has closely tracked the damaging impact of migration on housing costs' as a credibility marker, citing the outlet's own prior coverage as supporting evidence."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report written with strong and consistent right-leaning framing throughout the author's own prose. The piece uncritically advances a pro-deportation, anti-immigration narrative using loaded language, one-sided sourcing, and dismissive characterizations of Democrats and their policies. The density and consistency of directional bias warrants a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own framing treats mass deportations as unambiguously beneficial, with the headline-style assertion 'Mass deportations = lower housing costs for Americans' presented as factual rather than contested; (2) The author describes Biden's immigration policy as a 'flood' of migrants and attributes rising costs directly and causally to those migrants without engaging any counterarguments or alternative economic explanations in their own prose; (3) Democrats are described as fueling 'chaos' in 'migration-swelled, crime-ridden' cities — these are the author's own characterizations, not attributed to sources, and they frame Democratic governance as destructive; (4) Seattle's mayor is characterized by the author as representing 'far-left Democrats,' a politically loaded descriptor that goes beyond neutral identification; (5) The author states that 'crime and drug deaths are also declining' and 'Americans' wages and wealth-generating productivity are rising' as direct results of Trump's deportation policies, asserting causal relationships without evidence or qualification in the author's own voice; (6) The author frames Trump's deportation policies as helping Trump 'rebut the Democrats' 2026 media-amplified affordability talking point,' framing Democratic concerns as mere talking points amplified by a biased media; (7) Source selection is entirely one-sided: all named sources (Kevin Lynn of U.S. Tech Workers, Scott Bessent, JD Vance, Scott Turner) are either Trump administration officials or immigration restriction advocates, with no contrary economic voices included; (8) The author references 'Breitbart News has closely tracked the damaging impact of migration on housing costs' as a credibility marker, citing the outlet's own prior coverage as supporting evidence.
